characters.name: Sherlock Holmes
characters.description: The famous consulting detective known for his deductive methods and expertise in solving crimes.

physical.appearance: Has a dolichocephalic skull with well-marked supra-orbital development

characters.name: Watson
characters.description: Holmes's companion and chronicler, a medical doctor who assists in his investigations.

characters.name: James Mortimer
characters.description: A country medical practitioner and man of science who seeks Holmes's assistance with a serious problem.

physical.appearance: Very tall, thin man with a long nose like a beak, keen grey eyes set closely together, gold-rimmed glasses, dingy frock-coat, frayed trousers, long back already bowed, walks with forward thrust of head

characters.name: Bertillon
characters.description: A scientific expert whom Mortimer considers the highest in Europe, above Holmes.

timeline.event: Dr. Mortimer visits Holmes and Watson but they miss him, leaving behind his walking stick
Holmes and Watson examine Mortimer's walking stick at breakfast and deduce information about him
Watson looks up Mortimer in the Medical Directory to verify Holmes's deductions
Dr. Mortimer returns to retrieve his stick and meets Holmes and Watson
Mortimer reveals he has a serious problem and seeks Holmes's assistance

settings.name: Holmes and Watson's lodgings
settings.description: A room with a breakfast table, hearth-rug, settee, window with recess, and Watson's medical shelf containing the Medical Directory. Holmes has a favorite corner of the settee.

settings.name: Charing Cross Hospital
settings.description: A London hospital where Mortimer worked as a house-surgeon from 1882 to 1884.

settings.name: Grimpen, Dartmoor, Devon
settings.description: The country location where Dr. Mortimer currently practices, serving as Medical Officer for the parishes of Grimpen, Thorsley, and High Barrow.

world_rules: Medical practitioners require specific qualifications (M.R.C.S.) to practice
Hospital staff hierarchy distinguishes between established staff and house-surgeons/house-physicians
Professional presentations are customarily given when doctors leave positions
Country practitioners typically do more walking than town practitioners

voice_profile.style_notes: Formal Victorian prose with detailed dialogue and systematic deductive reasoning sequences

plot_threads.thread: What is the serious and extraordinary problem that Dr. Mortimer needs Holmes's help with?
Why does Mortimer consider this problem serious enough to seek out the famous Sherlock Holmes?
